description: Sacred powers drawn from divine conviction — blessings, righteous judgment, unbreakable oaths, and the authority to command spirits.
abilities: Banish

Casting a spirit creature into a distant shadow plane. At its basic level, the banishment lasts an hour before the spirit reappears at its former location. With far greater effort, the banishment becomes permanent. Only works against powerful spirit entities if they are near death.
Blazing Avenger

Reciting a magical statement about one's ideal while holding a weapon, imbuing it with extraordinary power. The weapon glows faintly at all times and flares brighter when enemies draw near. Fiery Avenger may be cast upon it at will with no effort, and a critical strike with the weapon unleashes a Smite upon the target. Only one weapon may carry this enchantment at a time; casting it again cancels the previous one.
Compel Truth

Locking eyes with a nearby creature as the invoker's eyes glow like blue flames, gripping the target's mind. At full power, the target is compelled to answer all questions truthfully for several minutes. A strong result compels three truthful answers, while a partial success yields one. Failure lets the target sense the manipulation and resist, and a catastrophic failure turns the spell back on the caster, compelling them to answer a question truthfully instead.
Declare

Declaring a reason for intervening in a matter and steeling one's resolve, grounded in a personal ideal and the circumstances at hand. The declaration empowers the invoker to immediately strike a nearby foe, compel someone to explain themselves, or convince common folk to vacate the area.
Fiery Avenger

Speaking a chosen phrase of power to ignite a held weapon in magical flame of any color. While ablaze, the weapon casts light in the nearby area and strikes with increased force. The flame persists until the wielder fails an attack with the weapon, and may be dismissed at any time.
Lawbringer

Raising a hand to the sky and summoning a spectral warhammer into the grip, crackling with blue-green light and trailing a deep, reverberating sound in its wake. The hammer deals heavy damage as a one-handed weapon. On a critical strike, the hammer releases a crackling boom that also unleashes the Thunderous Word effect on the target. The hammer vanishes in smoke after one hour or when dismissed.
Liberate

Placing a hand on a creature and speaking a word of power, channeling the weight of devotion and resolve to permanently alleviate the target of a character flaw that the caster already knows about. The flaw is effectively cured and no longer affects the creature's behavior, and any guilt from past failings tied to that flaw is also relieved, as though the Forgive ability had also been cast.
Petition

Reciting a short, structured prayer during a rest to receive a divine boon. The petition must contain an address invoking a higher power, a humble request, and words of adulation. Upon completing the recitation, all of the petitioner's wounds are fully healed.
Rebuke

Uttering a righteous word or phrase toward a nearby creature that is approaching during its advance, before it comes within reach. A spectral clone of the invoker rushes forward, knocks the creature back, deals minor damage, and halts its advance. The clone then vanishes.
Sacrifice

Laying hands on a creature that has died and bringing it back to life, provided the corpse remains mostly intact. If the creature died of old age, a new lifespan of uncertain length is granted. Each use withers the caster's soul, requiring them to take on an additional character flaw and permanently diminishing their vitality. Should the caster's vitality be reduced to nothing through repeated use, they gain the Wraith ability and permanently enter an ethereal form. Using Sacrifice while already fully diminished permanently kills the caster.
Smite

Speaking a word or phrase of condemnation upon a nearby visible creature, engulfing the target in radiant flame for devastating damage. If the damage is enough to slay the creature, it explodes into ash, body and spirit permanently obliterated. Destroying a creature this way carries a small risk that the creature's spirit becomes a dark passenger in the caster's mind, lurking there to speak unbidden and observe the invoker's choices.
Vow

Creating a permanent, irrevocable bond in service of an ideal, expressed as devotion to a deity, a people, a cause, or something else. The vow must include a promise to actively serve the ideal, a person, place, or group to protect when nearby, and a past wrong to make amends for. Once made, the invoker gains the ability to use Invoke freely, the healing of Petition extends to nearby allies, and a faint glow appears in the invoker's eyes. Betraying the vow's promises carries a sacred cost: each serious betrayal brings a sharp pain in the heart and a permanent reduction to the invoker's vitality, though it cannot reduce them below a survivable threshold.
Wraith

Becoming a creature between two worlds. Whenever reduced to the brink of death, the invoker may instantly become ethereal, appearing translucent and ghostly while remaining visible. The ethereal form cannot be healed and is immune to all physical harm, but harmful magic strikes with doubled intensity and can still kill. The invoker may revert to normal form at will, though they cannot shift forms to dodge an attack already in motion.
